prompt:

Target declarations: CausalSmith.Substrate.ConvergingTogetherClt.norm_cexp_mul_I_sub_cexp_mul_I_le, CausalSmith.Substrate.ConvergingTogetherClt.tendsto_charFun_sub_le, CausalSmith.Substrate.ConvergingTogetherClt.tendsto_charFun_sub_le_L2

## Instructions
Work ONLY in file `/<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/ConvergingTogetherClt/CharFunBound.lean`. It currently compiles with exactly three `sorry`s. Replace each `sorry` with a real proof. Do NOT change any theorem statement, signature, name, or hypothesis. Do NOT add `axiom`/`admit`/`native_decide`. After editing, run `lake build CausalSmith.Substrate.ConvergingTogetherClt.CharFunBound` from `/<repo-root>/CausalSmith` and ensure it builds with NO errors and NO remaining `sorry` (style warnings about line length are OK but avoid >100-char lines). Use the lean-lsp MCP tools (lean_goal, lean_hover_info, lean_loogle, lean_leansearch, lean_multi_attempt) to discover exact lemma names/signatures.

The three goals:

1. `norm_cexp_mul_I_sub_cexp_mul_I_le (a b : ℝ) : ‖Complex.exp (↑a * Complex.I) - Complex.exp (↑b * Complex.I)‖ ≤ |a - b|`.
   Proof sketch: write `↑a * I = ↑b * I + ↑(a-b) * I` (push_cast/ring_nf inside `Complex.exp`), so `cexp(↑a*I) = cexp(↑b*I) * cexp(↑(a-b)*I)` via `Complex.exp_add`. Then `cexp(↑a*I) - cexp(↑b*I) = cexp(↑b*I) * (cexp(↑(a-b)*I) - 1)`. Take norms: `‖cexp(↑b*I)‖ = 1` (lemma `Complex.norm_exp_ofReal_mul_I`, or `Complex.norm_exp_I_mul_ofReal` after `mul_comm`), and `‖cexp(↑(a-b)*I) - 1‖ ≤ ‖a-b‖ = |a-b|` via `Real.norm_exp_I_mul_ofReal_sub_one_le` (it is stated as `‖cexp (I * ↑x) - 1‖ ≤ ‖x‖`, so use `mul_comm` to match `↑x * I`; `Real.norm_eq_abs` turns `‖a-b‖` into `|a-b|`). Combine with `norm_mul`.

2. `tendsto_charFun_sub_le (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] {S T : Ω → ℝ} (hS : Measurable S) (hT : Measurable T) (hint : Integrable (fun ω => S ω - T ω) μ) (t : ℝ) : ‖charFun (μ.map S) t - charFun (μ.map T) t‖ ≤ |t| * ∫ ω, |S ω - T ω| ∂μ`.
   Proof sketch: rewrite both `charFun` via `MeasureTheory.charFun_apply_real` (`charFun ν t = ∫ x, Complex.exp (↑t*↑x*I) ∂ν`), then push through the pushforward with `MeasureTheory.integral_map hS.aemeasurable (f := fun x => Complex.exp (↑t*↑x*I))` (the integrand is continuous, discharge `AEStronglyMeasurable` by `Continuous.aestronglyMeasurable`/`by fun_prop`); similarly for T. Each function `gS := fun ω => Complex.exp (↑t*↑(S ω)*I)` and `gT` is Integrable on the probability measure because its norm is `1` (use `MeasureTheory.Integrable.mono'` against `integrable_const (1:ℝ)`, with AEStronglyMeasurable from `(Complex.continuous_exp.comp (by fun_prop)).aestronglyMeasurable`, or `MeasureTheory.integrable_of_forall_norm_le`/bounded; `‖Complex.exp (z*I)‖`-type norms are `1` by `Complex.norm_exp_ofReal_mul_I`). Then `charFun(μ.map S) t - charFun(μ.map T) t = ∫ ω, (gS ω - gT ω) ∂μ` by `MeasureTheory.integral_sub (gS integ) (gT integ)` reversed. Bound: `‖∫ (gS-gT)‖ ≤ ∫ ‖gS ω - gT ω‖ ∂μ` (`MeasureTheory.norm_integral_le_integral_norm`). Pointwise `‖gS ω - gT ω‖ ≤ |t*S ω - t*T ω| = |t|*|S ω - T ω|` using `norm_cexp_mul_I_sub_cexp_mul_I_le (t*S ω) (t*T ω)` after rewriting `↑t*↑(S ω) = ↑(t*S ω)` (push_cast) and `|t*S ω - t*T ω| = |t * (S ω - T ω)| = |t|*|S ω - T ω|` (`mul_sub`, `abs_mul`). Finish with `MeasureTheory.integral_mono` (needs both integrands integrable: `∫‖gS-gT‖` integrable since `gS-gT` integrable; `fun ω => |t|*|S ω - T ω|` integrable from `hint` via `Integrable.abs` and `Integrable.const_mul`) and `MeasureTheory.integral_const_mul` to pull out `|t|`. Use `MeasureTheory.integral_abs`/`hint.abs` for `∫|S-T|`.

3. `tendsto_charFun_sub_le_L2 (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] {S T : Ω → ℝ} (hS : MemLp S 2 μ) (hT : MemLp T 2 μ) (t : ℝ) : ‖charFun (μ.map S) t - charFun (μ.map T) t‖ ≤ |t| * Real.sqrt (∫ ω, (S ω - T ω) ^ 2 ∂μ)`.
   Proof sketch: From `hS.sub hT : MemLp (fun ω => S ω - T ω) 2 μ`, get `Integrable (S-T) μ` via `(hS.sub hT).integrable (by norm_num : (1:ℝ≥0∞) ≤ 2)`; also `Measurable (S-T)` from `hS.aestronglyMeasurable`/measurability — or obtain measurability from MemLp via `.aestronglyMeasurable.measurable`? prefer to require `Measurable` from `MemLp.aestronglyMeasurable` is only AEStronglyMeasurable; instead note `tendsto_charFun_sub_le` needs `Measurable S`,`Measurable T`. If you cannot get genuine `Measurable` from `MemLp`, restructure: prove an intermediate `∫|S-T| ≤ √(∫(S-T)²)` and bound `‖charFun diff‖` by re-deriving the L¹ step at the `AEStronglyMeasurable` level. SIMPLER: the cleanest path is to apply Cauchy–Schwarz to get `∫ ω, |S ω - T ω| ∂μ ≤ Real.sqrt (∫ ω, (S ω - T ω)^2 ∂μ)`, then chain with `tendsto_charFun_sub_le` — BUT that needs `Measurable S/T`. Since this lemma's hypotheses give only `MemLp`, EITHER (a) add nothing and use that `MemLp` for ℝ-valued functions on a Borel space typically comes with measurability via `AEMeasurable`; or (b) note that `charFun (μ.map S)` only depends on the law and AEStronglyMeasurable suffices for `integral_map` (`MeasureTheory.integral_map` needs `AEMeasurable`). Recommended: prove a private helper `tendsto_charFun_sub_le'` taking `AEMeasurable S`,`AEMeasurable T`,`Integrable (S-T)` instead of `Measurable`, reusing the exact proof of `tendsto_charFun_sub_le` (use `hS.aestronglyMeasurable.aemeasurable`). Then derive both `tendsto_charFun_sub_le_L2` and keep `tendsto_charFun_sub_le` (the latter from the former helper via `hS.aemeasurable`). DO NOT change the public signatures. For the Cauchy–Schwarz `∫|S-T| ≤ √(∫(S-T)²)`: model it on the private lemma `abs_integral_mul_le_sqrt` in `/<repo-root>/Causalean/Mathlib/Probability/SteinMethod/DependencyCLT.lean` (it proves `|∫ f*g| ≤ √(∫f²)·√(∫g²)` via `integral_mul_norm_le_Lp_mul_Lq` with HolderConjugate 2 2). Apply with `f := fun ω => S ω - T ω`, `g := fun _ => (1:ℝ)`: then `∫|S-T| = ∫|(S-T)*1| ≤ √(∫(S-T)²)·√(∫1²)` and `∫(1:ℝ)² = 1` on the probability measure (`integral_const`, `measure_univ`), `Real.sqrt 1 = 1`. Read that DependencyCLT file first to copy the exact API calls.

IMPORTANT: explore the actual goal states with lean_goal at each step and verify final build is clean.
